title: 有了 Vibe Coding, 第一次发现 vscode tunnel 模式这么有用
content: Vibe Coding allows developers to use VS Code’s tunnel mode, which is comparable to SSH for remote work but often suffers from latency in China due to lack of local relay servers. In Vibe Coding environments, lower latency reduces the need for tunnels, turning the relay into an advantage for accessing internal machines. The author previously had limited options without a public IP but, after acquiring a VM through a Kimi subscription, found a clear use case for tunnel mode.

title: 我做了一个开源 AI 语音平台 — Feros.ai
content: Feros.ai is an open‑source AI voice platform built by its developer for enterprise use. It offers fully self‑hosted, Rust‑based voice tools with low latency, allowing users to switch between multiple LLM, TTS, and STT models. The platform supports custom voice‑agent workflows for customer service, appointments, FAQs, and surveys, and can integrate with phone systems, web interfaces, and automation tools. It is production‑ready and available on GitHub for teams to deploy and modify.

content: Programmers often buy new hardware early to improve their workflow. Popular items include high‑end CPUs, GPUs, large‑capacity SSDs, 4K or higher resolution monitors, ergonomic chairs, adjustable desks, and premium keyboards. Some also invest in fast networking setups, like gigabit broadband and quality cables. Overall, the trend is to upgrade any equipment that can reduce development time, enhance ergonomics, or provide better performance for future projects.

content: Erin Price‑Wright interviews Turner Caldwell and Drew Baglino on closing the U.S. critical‑minerals gap and modernizing grid infrastructure needed for AI. They highlight the U.S.’s 50‑year lag behind China, the bottlenecks in mining, refining, and supply chains, and how automation, AI, and autonomous systems can shorten timelines. The discussion covers solid‑state transformers, autonomous mineral processing, and policy measures like permitting, federal grid investment, and a mission‑driven industry approach.

title: 不吐不快， cursor 在变相折磨老用户
content: Long‑term users of the Opus4.6 API frequently hit a throttling rule: if a single request exceeds roughly 5 million tokens, the cursor counts twice and all further messages in that session fail with “take longer than expected.” The only way to resume conversation is to start a new chat and send a trivial prompt, which temporarily restores access. This appears to be a deliberate strategy targeting heavy‑usage users, requiring multiple small interactions to unlock the next large request.

title: 分享一下使用 Claude Code 和 ChatGPT 不被封的经验。
content: The team upgraded to €200 subscriptions for Claude and GPT, using GPT for architecture and testing, while Claude handles coding via its native app and VS Code plugin. Two colleagues were previously banned but not since adjusting settings. Key lessons: use the tools only for coding to avoid bans linked to illicit tools; avoid frequent IP changes or sharing one IP—each person keeps a single hosting IP; pay with a unique virtual credit card whose billing address matches exactly the address on the platforms; limit each account to three devices that share the same IP to reduce the risk of account sharing; when a ban occurs the problem involves IP, software, and hardware configurations, not only the IP.

title: 大白话聊聊，AIDC里面的备电（BBU、UPS、柴发）
content: Data centers powering AI GPUs need three levels of backup power: a battery stabilizer (BBU) that handles second‑level spikes, a UPS that buffers for minutes, and diesel generators for hours. GPUs draw power in a jagged, millisecond‑scale pattern, causing severe voltage ripple that can damage or miscompute. The BBU now acts as a high‑voltage (≈800 V DC) power‑shape controller, the UPS is shifting from low‑voltage 400 V DC to medium‑voltage 10–35 kV DC, and generators provide long‑term continuity. This layered system smooths power, protects GPUs, and keeps data‑center operations stable.

title: The vi family
content: The vi family refers to a group of text editors that share a common heritage with the original vi editor. These descendants—such as vim, nvi, elvis, and others—retain vi’s modal editing model while adding features like syntax highlighting, scripting, and improved usability. Each variant extends the core design, offering distinct enhancements but maintaining compatibility with vi’s commands and configuration conventions.

title: Zero-native – Build native desktop apps with web UI
content: “Zero-native” is a framework that lets developers create native desktop applications while using web technologies for the user interface. It bridges web UI components with native platform capabilities, enabling a single codebase to run on different desktop operating systems. The approach reduces the need for separate native codebases, streamlining development and deployment.

title: 父亲带娃在迪士尼排队3小时遭优速通「插队」崩溃，园方有哪些更好解决办法？哪些领域应保留「金钱不干预」？
content: During the May Day period, a father and his daughter waited in line for roughly three hours at Disneyland.  He was frustrated when “优速通” ticket holders used a dedicated entrance to bypass the queue, prompting him to accuse staff of allowing money to dictate priority.  Staff said it was a park policy.  Critics argue the system disadvantages ordinary visitors and exposes children to class inequality, while supporters see it as a legitimate tiered service similar to airline business class.  In response, Disney altered its rules, separating the quick‑pass and regular lines, but the wait times for normal ticket holders increased.

title: vibecoding 的前提下，对编程语言的选择是否会变化？
content: With the rise of vio‑coding, language choice criteria shift. Familiarity becomes less critical thanks to AI. Strongly typed languages like Rust, offering rigorous checks, gain advantage for AI‑driven development. Weakly typed, developer‑friendly languages such as JavaScript or Python may hinder AI output quality. Additionally, AI lowers the cost of creating new libraries, reducing dependence on a language’s existing ecosystem; tools can generate entire frontend stacks with minimal effort. The community now questions whether language selection remains a major concern.

title: 做了个能用自然语言管理文件的 Android App，聊聊端侧 CLIP 模型选型和 Agent 架构
content: I built “PureFlow,” an ad‑free Android file manager that uses on‑device AI for natural‑language file tasks. It features fast, multi‑tab browsing, native ePub/PDF reading, and zero tracking. Core innovations include a mobile CLIP model for visual search coupled with a small Chinese‑to‑English translation model, enabling fast semantic queries; an agent that plans actions via a cloud LLM (API key supplied by the user) but executes all file operations locally; and a vector‑distance method for detecting near‑duplicate images. The app is open source and available free on Google Play.

title: 三星电子与工会彻夜谈判破裂，5 万工人即将罢工，双方有何核心矛盾？此次待遇之争折射出哪些深层问题？
content: Samsung Electronics and its union failed to reach a deal after overnight talks, leaving over 50,000 workers poised to strike on May 21 for 18 days. The dispute centers on a 50% annual wage‑bonus cap, which the union wants abolished and replaced by a 15% share of operating profit in employee bonuses. Samsung proposes 10% plus a one‑time payment. The failure reflects deeper grievances about profit sharing, competitive inequalities with SK Hynix, and the impact on global memory‑chip supply and prices.

title: OpenAI 前 CTO 创业大模型首秀，第一剑先斩 ChatGPT 聊天框
content: Thinking Machines, led by former OpenAI CTO Mira Murati, released a new real‑time interaction model that keeps the AI “present” by processing input and output in 200 ms micro‑turns. The system uses a front‑end perception model paired with a back‑end reasoning engine, allowing simultaneous speaking, interjections, tool calls, and visual tracking. In benchmarks it outperforms GPT‑Realtime‑2.0 and Gemini on interruption and background‑noise tests while matching or exceeding them on deep‑reasoning tasks. The approach aims to make human‑AI collaboration feel conversational rather than chat‑box‑based.

content: eBay stated that it trusts its current management team to continue leading the company and drive growth. The news caused eBay’s stock to fall about 1% in premarket trading, while GameStop’s shares dropped roughly 5%.

The post outlines a pragmatic trend‑trading rule: reduce exposure to high‑volatility positions and increase holdings in low‑volatility ones. Most traders lack the skill to identify key turning points or core sector drivers, leading to over‑reactive short‑term moves that hurt long‑term trend returns. By selling after sharp gains and buying at calmer levels—especially after large intraday rises or pullbacks—traders can temper volatility, compensate for missed upside, and follow the underlying trend more steadily.

title: 阿尔特曼出庭为个人领导力及巨额投资辩护
content: Elon Musk has sued OpenAI, alleging that Sam Altman and the organization breached a charitable trust and profited improperly. Musk claims that while OpenAI was a non‑profit, he was persuaded to donate millions of dollars, but Altman subsequently converted the entity into a for‑profit company. The lawsuit also challenges Altman’s personal investment actions and leadership decisions. The case is currently under trial in court.

title: DeepSeek 回应上线聊天记录搜索功能：正在灰度测试中
content: DeepSeek has added keyword search for chat history on both its web and mobile apps (version 2.1.0). Users can locate past conversations via a search box in the sidebar or a magnifying‑glass button. The feature is currently in a gradual rollout; those who do not see it can verify that they are running the latest app version.

title: 市场监管总局附条件批准腾讯收购喜马拉雅股权案
content: The State Administration for Market Regulation announced that it has conditionally approved Tencent’s acquisition of a stake in Ximalaya. It found that the deal could restrict competition in China’s online audio and music streaming market. Therefore, Tencent, Ximalaya, and the post‑merger entity must make five restrictive commitments. The authorities believe these measures will address competition concerns and protect stakeholder interests, and they will enforce the commitments to maintain fair market competition.

title: 上海 AI 实验室联合团队攻克芯片核心材料光刻胶稳定制备难题
content: Shanghai AI Laboratory, together with Xiamen University and the Suzhou National Laboratory, used the “Shusheng” scientific large model to create an AI‑driven automated synthesis loop for KrF resist resins. This closed‑loop system achieves high purity, consistency, and efficiency, enabling domestic production of high‑end lithography resist without relying on foreign suppliers. The platform has produced multiple automated batches with improved batch‑to‑batch consistency, met key industrial performance targets, and will advance to client-side validation.

title: 迫于巨大的 KPI 压力，亚马逊员工让 AI 工具「无效瞎忙」
content: Amazon employees, under heavy KPI pressure, used the internal AI tool MeshClaw to generate excessive, unnecessary AI activity to demonstrate "high-frequency use." They inflated token consumption, while management supposedly did not base performance on this data, employees still felt monitored. Concerns were raised about the tool’s need for user permissions and potential security risks if an AI agent errs. The incident highlights internal misuse of AI driven by metrics rather than genuine productivity.

title: 谷歌：首次发现黑客借助 AI 开发「零日」漏洞攻击工具
content: Google security researchers discovered that a cybercrime group used artificial intelligence to develop a tool capable of bypassing a widely deployed system management defense. After contacting the tool’s developers, Google thwarted the attack. This marks the first time Google’s threat intelligence team has identified "zero‑day" vulnerabilities generated by AI. The company confirmed AI was employed to find and weaponize the flaw but did not disclose the criminal group, affected software, the specific language model used, or the details of the vulnerability.

title: 恒瑞医药：与百时美施贵宝公司签署战略合作及许可协议
content: Hengrui Medicine announced a global strategic partnership and licensing agreement with Bristol‑Myers Squibb (BMS). The deal covers 13 early‑stage projects in oncology, hematology, and immunology. BMS will make payments up to $950 million, and the overall transaction could be worth approximately $15.2 billion.

title: 泡泡玛特跨界卖茶饮？知情人士：POP BAKERY 直营门店仍以甜品为主
content: PopMat’s dessert brand POP BAKERY is opening its first directly operated store in Qinhuangdao’s Anaya area, adding a tea‑drink section while keeping desserts as the core focus. The chain plans to expand into own coffee and beverage offerings as its pop‑up model proves successful. PopMat, originally known for blind‑box toys, has broadened into various areas and projected strong 2025 revenue growth, though competition in the toy market is intensifying and its stock has slid after UBS revised its forecasts.

title: 沪上阿姨企业账号发有偿陪睡信息引热议 官方回应：账号疑被盗用 现已立案
content: A Guangzhou-based caller alleges that a WeChat corporate account of the Shanghai Aunties service sent a harassment message offering paid companionship for 5,000 yuan. Staff at the store say the account was likely compromised. On May 12, the company informed authorities, provided monitoring footage and login data, and declared that the malicious posts were not from its employees. Police opened a case, and the business has apologized to the affected client.

title: GitHub Copilot individual plans: Introducing flex allotments in Pro and Pro+, and a new Max plan
content: GitHub Copilot will switch to usage‑based billing on June 1. The individual plans—Free, Pro, Pro+, and a new Max—will provide more included usage. Pro ($10/mo) gets $15 total credits, Pro+ ($39/mo) gets $70, and Max ($100/mo) gets $200. Base credits equal the subscription price and always stay the same; a variable “flex” allotment adds extra usage that can change over time. Unlimited code completions remain free on paid plans; excess usage can be purchased.

title: 一个ai输家写给自己的信
content: This letter reflects on the author’s reluctance to record thoughts that may later feel like “trying to make bitter grapes seem sweet.” The writer discusses how technologies can be bubbles either when rejected or accepted, and how asset value can diverge from reality due to supply–demand shifts. He compares AI evaluation to investment criteria—speed, cost, quality—and highlights China’s potential to develop large‑scale AI models despite current censorship. Finally, he muses on the nature of reality as a simulation and the value of having the chance to think and document.
